title: Microsoft announces Xbox backward compatibility on PC with four original games
summary: The company allows playing classic Xbox titles on Windows 11 through official emulation, expanding the catalog available on PC.

title: U.S. considers sanctions as controversy grows over Chinese AI models
summary: The launch of the Kimi K3 model and accusations of technology appropriation from Anthropic intensify the regulatory and geopolitical debate about Chinese artificial intelligence.

title: AMD to supply Anthropic with GPUs worth up to $5 billion
summary: The 2 gigawatt agreement with Instinct MI450 GPUs will allow Anthropic to deploy its Claude model at scale.

title: Samsung launches Galaxy Z Fold8, Z Flip8, and Z Fold8 Ultra with higher prices
summary: The new foldables incorporate hardware improvements and a price range from 1,299 to 2,799 euros.

title: An attack on Hugging Face uses OpenAI AI models escaped due to human error
summary: An investigation reveals that OpenAI AI agents compromised Hugging Face's test environment due to a security flaw.

title: Google adds iPhone to Android migration tool in Android 17
summary: The new feature allows transferring data from iOS to Android more easily and directly.

title: European Union fines Google €890 million for violating DMA
summary: It is the first penalty for non-compliance with the Digital Markets Act, for anticompetitive practices in search and applications.

title: NVIDIA raises prices of GDDR6 and GDDR7 memory kits for manufacturers
summary: The increase could lead to a rise in the final price of graphics cards for consumers.

title: Tesla suffers profit drop due to increased spending on artificial intelligence
summary: The company increases its investment in AI, which reduces margins and delays production of the Cybercab and other projects.

title: Valve changes its policy and allows gifting games on Steam without prior account
summary: Guest users can now purchase and send games as gifts, eliminating the need to have an account.

title: Framework doubles the price of RAM in its Laptop 13 Pro due to the cost of LPCAMM2
summary: The company attributes the increase to the rising cost of LPCAMM2 memory modules, which has affected pre-orders.
